prompt:
You are Agent 1 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-N2dtjt.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Design and implement the foundation of a notification system.

Specifically:
1. Choose an architecture for notifications: event-driven (EventBus with subscribers) OR direct service calls. Commit to ONE approach.
2. Define the notification service interface and core types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.) in src/services/ and src/models/.
3. Implement the base notification service with the chosen architecture.
4. Create an email notification adapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (stub is fine, but interface must be real).
5. Document your architectural decision — the pattern you chose, why, and what constraints it imposes.
6. Make sure the codebase compiles.

Important:
- Pick ONE pattern (event-driven OR direct calls) and commit to it. Do not mix approaches.
- Define clear interfaces that other developers will implement against.
- Document constraints: e.g., "All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface" or "All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()".
- 11 more developers will build on your foundation.

parameters.task: Design and implement notification system foundation: choose event-driven vs direct calls architecture, define core types/interfaces, implement base notification service, create email adapter stub

parameters.summary: Notification system uses event-driven architecture via EventBus exclusively
parameters.context: The codebase has two communication patterns: EventBus (event-driven) and CallbackRegistry (direct callbacks). The existing NotificationService mixes both, which is noted as inconsistent. Need to pick ONE pattern for the notification system foundation that 11 other developers will build on.
parameters.rationale: EventBus is the right choice because: (1) it's already the primary architecture declared in the codebase docs and index.ts, (2) it provides loose coupling — new notification channels can be added without modifying emitters, (3) it has an event log for debugging/testing, (4) it supports async handlers natively, (5) typed events provide compile-time safety. The CallbackRegistry usage for order:status-changed will be migrated to EventBus for consistency.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Tighter coupling, no event log, synchronous-only, inconsistent with the rest of the codebase. Would require callers to know about notification internals.

constraints: All notifications must flow through EventBus.emit() — no direct service calls for notifications
All delivery channels must implement the NotificationAdapter interface
NotificationService subscribes to domain events and dispatches to registered adapters
Adapters are registered at startup, not discovered dynamically

parameters.old_string:
import { statusChangeCallbacks } from '../utils/callback-registry.js';
import { Logger } from '../utils/logger.js';

parameters.command:
git add src/models/notification.ts src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts src/adapters/email.adapter.ts src/services/notification.service.ts src/services/order.service.ts src/index.ts tests/services/notification.service.test.ts tests/services/order.service.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Implement event-driven notification system foundation

Unify notification architecture on EventBus exclusively, eliminating the
mixed CallbackRegistry/EventBus pattern. Define NotificationAdapter interface
that all delivery channels must implement, with EmailAdapter as the first
concrete adapter. NotificationService subscribes to domain events and
dispatches to registered adapters.

Key design decisions:
- All notifications flow through EventBus.emit() — no direct service calls
- All adapters implement NotificationAdapter interface (channel, send method)
- OrderService now emits order:status-changed via EventBus (was CallbackRegistry)
- NotificationService maps domain events to NotificationPayloads and routes
  to the appropriate adapter by channel

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

parameters.summary: Agent 1: Notification system foundation implemented — event-driven via EventBus, adapter pattern for delivery channels
parameters.detail:
Completed notification system foundation:

## Architecture: Event-driven via EventBus (exclusively)
- Eliminated CallbackRegistry usage from NotificationService and OrderService
- All domain events (order:created, order:status-changed, user:created) flow through EventBus
- NotificationService subscribes to events and dispatches to registered adapters

## New files
- `src/models/notification.ts` — Core types: NotificationType, NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, NotificationRecord
- `src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts` — NotificationAdapter interface (channel + send method)
- `src/adapters/email.adapter.ts` — EmailAdapter stub implementing the interface

## Modified files
- `src/services/notification.service.ts` — Rewritten to use adapters + EventBus only
- `src/services/order.service.ts` — Status changes now emit via EventBus (was CallbackRegistry)
- `src/index.ts` — Exports new types and adapters

## Constraints for other developers
1. All notifications MUST go through EventBus.emit() — no direct service calls
2. All delivery channels MUST implement NotificationAdapter interface
3. Adapters are registered with NotificationService.registerAdapter() at startup
4. Adapters must not throw — return failed NotificationResult instead
5. One adapter per channel (later registration replaces earlier)

## Tests: 72/72 passing, build compiles clean

fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ec5eb7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Project Guidelines
+
+## Architecture
+- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
+- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
+- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
+- All business logic lives in the service layer
+
+## Coding Conventions
+- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
+- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
+- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
+- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
+- Prefer async/await over raw Promises
+
+## Testing
+- Tests use vitest
+- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
+- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
+- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method
+
+## File Organization
+- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
+- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
+- src/services/ — Business logic layer
+- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
+- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
+- src/config/ — Configuration files
+- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure
+
+## Git Practices
+- Commit atomically per logical change
+- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
+- Run tests before committing

diff --git a/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..a2be537
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,29 @@
+/**
+ * NotificationAdapter interface — the contract all delivery channel adapters must implement.
+ *
+ * Constraint: Every notification delivery channel (email, webhook, SMS, in-app)
+ * must implement this interface and be registered with the NotificationService.
+ *
+ * Adapters are responsible for:
+ * 1. Delivering the notification via their specific channel
+ * 2. Returning a NotificationResult indicating success or failure
+ * 3. Handling their own retries and error management internally
+ *
+ * Adapters must NOT:
+ * - Emit events on the EventBus (that's the NotificationService's job)
+ * - Access other adapters or services directly
+ * - Modify the NotificationPayload they receive
+ */
+
+import type { NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult } from '../models/notification.js';
+
+export interface NotificationAdapter {
+  /** The channel this adapter handles */
+  readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+
+  /**
+   * Send a notification through this adapter's channel.
+   * Must not throw — return a failed NotificationResult instead.
+   */
+  send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+}
